reasoning: A massive anti-government protest (following the arrest of Ekrem İmamoğlu) started in Turkey on March 19, 2025, with an estimated 300,000 participants. This protest perfectly matches the inclusion criteria for Carnegie's Global Protest Tracker (an anti-government protest surpassing 10,000 participants). The only uncertainty is whether Carnegie's database will be updated to include it by the July 1, 2025 resolution date. Snippets suggest the tracker was last updated in November 2024, meaning it had not been updated for about six months as of May 2025. Given that it typically receives periodic updates (likely semi-annually or a few times a year), there is a coin-flip to slightly favorable chance that a scheduled update occurs and captures the Turkish protests before the July 1 cutoff.
